div.h2: PTRACE_EVENT_EXEC: Memory Map Replacement and Per-PID State Reset
div.p: PID 1234 calls execve() — kernel replaces entire address space before first instruction of new program

g.text: Why in_syscall = 1 is KEPT (not reset to 0)

text.tspan: /* Keep in_syscall = 1: exec exit stop is still coming.
* Resetting to 0 would desync the toggle permanently. */
/* Discard stale partial line — old address space is gone;
* any pointers in outbuf are meaningless. */
/* Zero all cached arg values — they pointed into the old
* address space; PTRACE_PEEKDATA would return EFAULT. */
/* In handle_syscall_stop() — exit stop with outbuf_len == 0: */
/* Mid-exec or mid-attach: no entry half was buffered.

g.text: return; /* skip stats_record — entry_time was not set cleanly */

text.tspan: /* Keep in_syscall = 1: exec exit stop is still coming.
* Resetting to 0 would desync the toggle permanently. */
/* Discard stale partial line — old address space is gone;
* any pointers in outbuf are meaningless. */
/* Zero all cached arg values — they pointed into the old
* address space; PTRACE_PEEKDATA would return EFAULT. */
/* In handle_syscall_stop() — exit stop with outbuf_len == 0: */
/* Mid-exec or mid-attach: no entry half was buffered.
/* skip stats_record — entry_time was not set cleanly */

div.p: ⚠️ ALL pointer-valued args are STALE after exec The old page table is destroyed. PTRACE_PEEKDATA on these addresses → EFAULT The virtual addresses may be reused by the NEW program for completely different data — or not mapped at all.

g.text: 1. mm_release(): drops all VMAs from old mm_struct
pid_state_t — same slot, fields reset by handle_exec_event()
offset 0x08 in_syscall = 1 ← KEPT at 1 (exec exit stop pending)
offset 0x0C outbuf_len = 0 ← RESET: stale partial line discarded
offset 0x50 entry_time = unchanged (irrelevant; outbuf_len==0 skips stats)

p.strong: ⑤ exec syscall-exit stop (next stop after resuming):

ul.li: outbuf_len == 0 → skip print (<... execve resumed> = 0 path)

text.tspan: Exit stop sees in_syscall==0 → flips to 1
→ tracer thinks NEXT stop is an EXIT
→ reads args as retval → GARBAGE output

g.text: args[1] = 0x7fff_ab12_3480 ← ptr to argv[] OLD VADDR
args[2] = 0x7fff_ab12_3500 ← ptr to envp[] OLD VADDR
